place: Vineland city, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Vineland earned the nickname "The Egg Basket of America" as poultry farming dominated its economy in the early 20th century.
The Vineland Historical and Antiquarian Society, the state's oldest purpose‑built museum, opened in 1910 and still houses a large collection of local history.
The West Jersey and Seashore Railroad once ran through Vineland, linking Camden to Cape May and shaping the town’s early growth.
The annual Vineland Produce Auction, the largest on the east coast, serves as a hub for fresh fruits and vegetables and draws regional buyers.
